mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 4% signals stronger labor market conditions, boosting corporate earnings and consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 4%.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects expanding economic activity, which raises corporate profits and stock valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to outperform baseline returns in 50%+ of quarters with GDP growth above 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates robust supply-side capacity and labor demand, creating positive momentum in equity markets over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction between INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to generate positive returns in 40%+ of co-moving instances.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in CPI inflation above 3% triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, which causes negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPI exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 5% during a financial crisis signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer confidence and corporate earnings, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 50%+ of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stronger manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate earnings and stock prices, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to generate positive 5d returns in 40%+ of INDPRO growth instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal coordinated monetary and labor market easing, creating a strong tailwind for equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction between FEDFUNDS and UNRATE fails to generate positive returns in 30%+ of aligned instances.
